steps.title: Spot the contrast in the notes
steps.content: The notes provide both a general claim—“Most circumnavigations include stops on land”—and specific exceptions (Moreau and Spencer's nonstop circumnavigations). A generalization must use both to show the range of circumnavigation types.

steps.title: Choose the answer combining both patterns
steps.content:
Choice A combines the general principle and the exceptions: most circumnavigations include stops, but some are nonstop.

This uses multiple notes to reveal how circumnavigations vary.

The other three options either define the term, list specific details, or mention the sailors without drawing broader conclusions.

That is the correct answer.
